Class DefaultGoalChooser

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.prover.impl.DefaultGoalChooser
All Implemented Interfaces:
GoalChooser<@Nullable Proof, @Nullable Goal>
Direct Known Subclasses:
DepthFirstGoalChooser
public class DefaultGoalChooser extends Object implements GoalChooser<@Nullable Proof, @Nullable Goal>
Helper class for managing a list of goals on which rules are applied. The class provides methods for removing a goal, and for updating the internal data structures after a rule has been applied.

    Method
    Description
    protected void
    findMinimalSubtree(@NonNull Node p_startNode)
    Search for a minimal subtree of the proof tree which is not closable (constraints of its goals are inconsistent) starting at p_startNode
    PRECONDITION: all goals have satisfiable constraints
    protected boolean

    Search for a minimal subtree of the proof tree which is not closable (constraints of its goals are inconsistent) below p_startNode

    • proof

      protected @Nullable Proof proof
      the proof that is worked with

    • goalList

      protected ImmutableList<Goal> goalList
      list of goals on which the strategy should be applied

    • nextGoals

      protected ImmutableList<Goal> nextGoals
      part of goalList that should be worked on next

    • allGoalsSatisfiable

      protected boolean allGoalsSatisfiable
      true iff all goals have satisfiable constraints

    • selectedList

      protected ImmutableList<Goal> selectedList
      Subset of the goals to which currently taclets are applied. First this is the list of goals with unsatisfiable constraints, later this is a subset of the goals having inconsistent constraints

    • currentSubtreeRoot

      protected Node currentSubtreeRoot

    • nextGoalCounter

      protected int nextGoalCounter

    • init

      public void init(@Nullable Proof p_proof, @Nullable ImmutableList<Goal> p_goals)

      Specified by:
      init in interface GoalChooser<@Nullable Proof, @Nullable Goal>

      p_proof -
      p_goals - the initial list of goals

      Returns:
      the next goal a taclet should be applied to

    • removeGoal

      public void removeGoal(Goal goal)

      Remove goal from selectedList (e.g. no taclet can be applied to goal)

    • updateGoalList

      public void updateGoalList(@Nullable Object node, @NonNull ImmutableList<Goal> newGoals)
      Description copied from interface: GoalChooser
      The given node has become an internal node of the proof tree, and the children of the node are the given goals
      Specified by:
      updateGoalList in interface GoalChooser<@Nullable Proof, @Nullable Goal>
      Parameters:
      node - the content of the goal which has been replaced by the new goals; it is used to identify outdated goals managed by this chooser (a typical argument is the node of proof tree which is now an inner node)
      newGoals - the goals associated with node's children

    • updateGoalListHelp

      protected void updateGoalListHelp(Object node, ImmutableList<Goal> newGoals)

      protected ImmutableList<Goal> insertNewGoals(ImmutableList<Goal> newGoals, ImmutableList<Goal> prevGoalList)

      protected static ImmutableList<Goal> rotateList(ImmutableList<Goal> p_list)

    • removeClosedGoals

      protected void removeClosedGoals()

    • findMinimalSubtreeBelow

      protected boolean findMinimalSubtreeBelow(@NonNull Node p_startNode)
      Search for a minimal subtree of the proof tree which is not closable (constraints of its goals are inconsistent) below p_startNode

      PRECONDITION: * !isSatisfiableSubtree ( p_startNode ) * all goals have satisfiable constraints

      Returns:
      true iff a non-empty subtree was found

    • findMinimalSubtree

      protected void findMinimalSubtree(@NonNull Node p_startNode)
      Search for a minimal subtree of the proof tree which is not closable (constraints of its goals are inconsistent) starting at p_startNode
      PRECONDITION: all goals have satisfiable constraints
      Parameters:
      p_startNode - the node from where to start the search

    • isSatisfiableSubtree

      protected boolean isSatisfiableSubtree(@NonNull Node p_root)
